reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASSED - Function streamDataColumnBatch is called from line 99 in dataColumnSidecarsByRangeRPCHandler, which is registered in rpc.go. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASSED - P2P RPC handler processes untrusted peer requests. Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASSED - The code at lines 164-165 does exit mid-block when quota==0, breaking the sidecar loop (L150) before completing all sidecars of a block. Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASSED - An attacker can trigger this via P2P by requesting a large range that exhausts quota mid-block, causing partial sidecar transmission for the final block. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json lines 1097-1113 property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-043 states 'All sidecars of a block must be included in by_range response if any sidecar is included.' The code violates this atomicity requirement. Gate 6 (Scope): PASSED - P2P network vulnerability in PeerDAS, Fusaka-specific. Severity: Original MEDIUM is appropriate for Prysm's 31% market share; data availability impact affects requesting peers but does not cause consensus splits or validator slashing. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by crafting a data_column_sidecars_by_range request with parameters that exhaust the quota (MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS) mid-block, causing the receiving Prysm node to obtain incomplete sidecar sets that fall below the PeerDAS reconstruction threshold, resulting in wasted bandwidth and failed data availability checks.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: All sidecars of a block must be included in by_range response if any sidecar is included.
